read_plan.preview: **Temporary Housing Pressure** describes the recurrent mismatch between short-term shelter demand and available beds in labor districts, crossing towns, and clinic-adjacent neighborhoods.

Auralis society leans toward a soft caste structure, where social standing is loosely tied to magical ability. This is not a rigid hierarchy of law — high-tier casters do not hold formal political office by virtue of the

Auralis embraces the full spectrum of [[Discipline|Disciplines]], including those that make other cultures uncomfortable. Experimentation with Mind and Soul disciplines is not openly banned and in certain scholarly or mi

The principal settlements of Auralis are [[Solhaven]] (the capital, built up coastal terraces on the western shore) and [[Emberfall]] (the interior research and agricultural center). Auralis has several additional port c
Solhaven approaches a million residents and is the most vertically stratified city in Solumora. Its terrace zones function as distinct social worlds stacked on each other.

Terravelle society values stability and efficiency above all. Where other cultures may chase magical prestige, Terravelle embraces the philosophy that the best spell is the one that accomplishes the task with the least e

Low to mid-tier casting is common and unremarkable. [[Grimoires]] here tend to favor Triangle and Square sigil shapes — fast, safe, reproducible, and affordable in [[Flux]]. [[Discipline|Disciplines]] with predictable fa

[[Terravelle]] and [[Auralis]] share a complicated relationship shaped by fundamentally opposed philosophies. Where Auralis pursues magical innovation and supremacy, Terravelle prizes restraint and function. The [[Equato

The principal settlements of Terravelle are [[Valdenmoor]] (the capital, in the central river valley), [[Ashford]] (the primary eastern port), and [[Greyveil]] (the northern waypoint at the edge of the settled belt). Doz

The numeric tier is the spell's **Flux handling requirement**. Each tier is roughly an order-of-magnitude jump in difficulty, capacity, stability, and precision. Every few tiers feels like a categorically different class
_Barely any Flux. The threshold most people never bother to formally test._
_A small, usable trickle. The first tier where deliberate spellwork is practical._
_The world average. The first tier where magic becomes genuinely useful in daily life._

A transaction between two registered individuals requires the consent of both parties — one initiates, one confirms, and the ledger writes. The process takes moments for anyone with basic Flux sensitivity and slightly lo

The Watts ledger predates both kingdoms. The earliest records of birth rune transactions in either kingdom's archives trace back further than coherent written history — the system was already running when the first settl

Some people carry physical tokens stamped with their birth rune mark as a convenience — not because the token holds value, but because presenting it speeds up the consent verification process in busy commercial environme

Watts are fiat currency in the strict sense — they are backed by nothing except the network's integrity and the collective agreement to treat them as valuable. There is no gold reserve, no Flux commodity, no state guaran

The guild structure runs the Auralis economy much the way it runs Terravelle's, with one difference that changes most of what follows: Auralis guilds are formally tier-stratified. The tracks available to you, the positio

Auralis's engineered low basin grows rice and leafy plants that prefer warmth and moisture. The western and eastern coasts supply saltwater fish. The result is a diet with more variety than [[Terravelle]]'s river valleys

Tier assessment in Auralis is not a quiet formality. The [[Religion and Belief|Ascendant Path]] registers assessments, not guild halls, and the ceremony is witnessed. For T0 and T1, the registration is the end of the eve

The Ascendant Path is Auralis's dominant religious and cultural framework, and its theology maps conveniently onto the social structure: Flux mastery is the highest spiritual pursuit, the [[Control Tier|Control Tiers]] r

The guild is the organizing unit of working life. Every trade with enough practitioners to have collective standing has one — construction, textile work, food preparation, river transport, basic scribing, animal handling

The river valleys produce wheat, barley, and river fish. Farming communities in the upland regions export grain toward the cities and the coast. The base diet — grain, legumes, root vegetables, preserved protein — is sta

Tier assessment happens between eight and twelve, at a guild hall or community meeting room. The result is recorded, filed, and largely treated as a vocational qualification rather than a statement about a person's worth

**[[Crestward Flux Institute]]** pursues cost reduction, parallel execution patterns, and methods to achieve higher-tier effects through coordinated low-tier practitioner teams. Leadership: [[Casten Miel]] ([[Control Tie

**[[Northern Terravelle Academy]]** emphasizes practical settlement-scale application and coordinated research on [[Rift Incursions|rift mechanics]]. The Academy operates independently of state control (through funding d

**Independent scholars** operate throughout both kingdoms, conducting collaborative research that neither kingdom prioritizes internally. [[Kedn Rast]] ([[Control Tier|T3]]) exemplifies this institutional model, conducti
**Care Triage Workflows** are district-level rules used by clinics and infirmaries to prioritize treatment when demand exceeds practitioner throughput.
Most triage systems in [[Clinical Care Systems]] now operate across three layers:
Triage officers combine clinical and civic inputs:
Common techniques used during surge periods:
**Queue Management Offices** are municipal-administrative units that stabilize public access during service strain by converting informal waiting lines into controlled priority systems.
During normal periods, most services run on relationship networks, guild referrals, and local habit. Under strain, those informal systems fail first.
Queue offices perform four practical functions:
Queue systems fail when priority rules are opaque or changed without notice.
